title: Autonomy-level trade-off (AL0-AL5)
text: trade event-rate reduction versus Compliance Index. default AL scenario mapping requires full sweep; demonstrate two AL points shipped example data.
code: al <- al_efficiency(   sim,   al_scenarios         = c("0" = "A-S00", "1" = "A-S01"),   compliance_threshold = 0.80,   n_bootstrap          = 200 ) al$tradeoff_table #> # A tibble: 2 × 10 #>      al scenario event_median event_ci_lo event_ci_hi event_reduction_pct #>   <int> <chr>           <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>               <dbl> #> 1     0 A-S00           0.341       0.328       0.353                0    #> 2     1 A-S01           0.272       0.260       0.290                6.91 #> # ℹ 4 more variables: compliance <dbl>, above_threshold <lgl>, al_ratio <dbl>, #> #   n_reps <int>

title: Autonomy-level trade-off (AL0-AL5)
code: plot_al_tradeoff(al) #> `height` was translated to `width`.

title: Autonomy-level trade-off (AL0-AL5)
text: optimal_al slot holds AL level highest event-rate reduction staying compliance threshold: Compliance violations ():
code: al$optimal_al #> [1] 1 al$compliance_violations #> # A tibble: 1 × 11 #>      al scenario event_median event_ci_lo event_ci_hi event_reduction_pct #>   <int> <chr>           <dbl>       <dbl>       <dbl>               <dbl> #> 1     0 A-S00           0.341       0.328       0.353                   0 #> # ℹ 5 more variables: compliance <dbl>, above_threshold <lgl>, al_ratio <dbl>, #> #   n_reps <int>, compliance_deficit <dbl>

title: Entity flow analysis
text: Stage2 (time_to_stage2) present shipped example, machinery generalises Stage1..Stage4 columns exist simulation output.
code: st <- stage_throughput(sim) st #> # A tibble: 4 × 7 #>   scenario stage      n median_time   q25   q75 completed_frac #>   <chr>    <chr>  <int>       <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>          <dbl> #> 1 A-S00    Stage2  4000         153    77  258              NA #> 2 A-S01    Stage2  4000         145    74  253              NA #> 3 A-S07    Stage2  4000         151    80  259              NA #> 4 A-S08    Stage2  4000         151    77  259.             NA

title: Entity flow analysis
code: detect_bottlenecks(st, threshold = 0.75) #> # A tibble: 1 × 8 #>   scenario stage      n median_time   q25   q75 completed_frac is_bottleneck #>   <chr>    <chr>  <int>       <dbl> <dbl> <dbl>          <dbl> <lgl>         #> 1 A-S00    Stage2  4000         153    77   258             NA TRUE

title: Entity flow analysis
code: plot_scenario_heatmap(   sim,   metrics = c("median_time_to_first_service",               "median_time_to_stage2") )

title: Getting started with dynasimR
code: sim <- load_example_data() print(sim) #>  #> ── dynasimR_data ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Scenarios: 4 #> • Profile: "Profile_A" #> • Summary rows: 200 #> • Entity events: 16000 #> • Loaded: "2026-05-04 18:14" #> • Path: /home/runner/work/_temp/Library/dynasimR/extdata

title: Getting started with dynasimR
code: km <- km_estimate(sim, endpoint = "stage2",                   stratify_by = "scenario") plot_km(km, title = "Time-to-stage-2") #> Warning: Removed 2 rows containing missing values or values outside the scale range #> (`geom_ribbon()`).

title: Getting started with dynasimR
code: pol <- policy_effect(sim,   policy_a_scenario = "A-S08",   policy_b_scenario = "A-S07",   n_bootstrap       = 200) cat(pol$narrative) #> Under policy A (scenario A-S08), an event-rate reduction of 7.4 percentage points (95\%-CI: -9 to -5.6) was observed versus policy B (scenario A-S07) (Wilcoxon test: W = 215, p < 0.001). The Compliance Index was higher under policy A (0.919 vs. 0.658).

title: Getting started with dynasimR
text: two AL points present shipped example data, machinery full AL sweep.
code: al <- al_efficiency(   sim,   al_scenarios         = c("0" = "A-S00", "1" = "A-S01"),   compliance_threshold = 0.80,   n_bootstrap          = 200 ) plot_al_tradeoff(al) #> `height` was translated to `width`.

title: Getting started with dynasimR
code: export_latex_table(   data     = pol$effect_sizes,   filename = "policy_table.tex",   caption  = "Policy effect sizes.",   label    = "policy" )

title: Getting started with dynasimR
code: launch_app()                                        # example data launch_app(data_dir = "~/my-simulation/data/raw")

title: Manuscript export workflow
text: generated .tex uses \botrule escapes </> character cells compatibility common publisher templates.
code: library(dynasimR) sim <- load_example_data() pol <- policy_effect(sim,   policy_a_scenario = "A-S08",   policy_b_scenario = "A-S07")  export_latex_table(   data     = pol$effect_sizes,   filename = "table_policy.tex",   caption  = "Policy effect sizes (Cohen's d and risk difference).",   label    = "policy",   note     = "n = 50 replications per scenario." )

title: Manuscript export workflow
code: km <- km_estimate(sim, endpoint = "stage2") export_figure(   plot      = plot_km(km),   filename  = "figure_km.pdf",   width_mm  = 174,          # single column   height_mm = 110 )

title: Manuscript export workflow
text: Given source manuscript.tex placeholders like [XX_POLICY_DELTA_EVENT], substitute one call:
code: reps <- fill_placeholders(   sim_data          = sim,   tex_file          = "manuscript.tex",   output_file       = "manuscript_filled.tex",   policy_a_scenario = "A-S08",   policy_b_scenario = "A-S07",   baseline_scenario = "A-S00" ) reps          # named character vector of substitutions performed

title: Policy effects: Policy A vs. Policy B
text: two Profile scenarios -S07 (policy B) -S08 (policy , priority severity) differ allocation policy.
code: pol <- policy_effect(   sim,   policy_a_scenario = "A-S08",   policy_b_scenario = "A-S07",   n_bootstrap       = 500 ) print(pol) #>  #> ── dynasimR_policy ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────── #> • Policy A: "A-S08" #> • Policy B: "A-S07" #> • n (reps): 50 #>  #> ── Delta event rate ── #>  #> # A tibble: 4 × 4 #>   group median_pct_points  ci_lo ci_hi #>   <chr>             <dbl>  <dbl> <dbl> #> 1 all               -7.37  -9.10 -5.53 #> 2 A                -10.6  -14.4  -7.00 #> 3 B                -11.3  -14.9  -7.65 #> 4 C                 -9.39 -16.1  -4.13 #> ── Narrative ── #> Under policy A (scenario A-S08), an event-rate reduction of 7.4 percentage points (95\%-CI: -9.1 to -5.5) was observed versus policy B (scenario A-S07) (Wilcoxon test: W = 215, p < 0.001). The Compliance Index was higher under policy A (0.919 vs. 0.658).

title: Policy effects: Policy A vs. Policy B
code: plot_policy(pol) #> `height` was translated to `width`.

title: Policy effects: Policy A vs. Policy B
text: narrative slot LaTeX-escaped string ready drop report: policy (scenario -S08), event-rate reduction 7.4 percentage points (95%-CI: -9.1 -5.5) observed versus policy B (scenario -S07) (Wilcoxon test: W = 215, p < 0.001). Compliance Index higher policy (0.919 vs. 0.658).
code: cat(pol$narrative)

title: Policy effects: Policy A vs. Policy B
code: pol$effect_sizes #> # A tibble: 3 × 2 #>   metric                  value #>   <chr>                   <dbl> #> 1 Cohen_d_event         -1.95   #> 2 Risk_Difference_event -0.0685 #> 3 NNT_surrogate         14.6

title: Authors and Citation
text: Raban . Heller. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Heller R (2026). dynasimR: Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis. R package version 0.1.0, https://github.com/r-heller/dynasimR.
code: @Manual{,   title = {dynasimR: Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis},   author = {Raban A. Heller},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.1.0},   url = {https://github.com/r-heller/dynasimR}, }

title: dynasimR - Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis
text: domain-neutral analysis visualisation layer discrete-event, agent-based node-actor simulation outputs. package schema-harmonised two output profiles (Profile Profile B) can analysed single API. Typical use cases include system entities (actors, nodes, agents) move states interact within spatial network environments — example flow analyses, state-transition studies, resource allocation policy comparisons.

title: dynasimR - Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis
code: # install.packages("remotes") remotes::install_github("r-heller/dynasimR")

title: dynasimR - Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis
code: library(dynasimR)  # 1. Load bundled example data (or point to your own simulation outputs) sim <- load_example_data() # sim <- read_simulation("~/my-simulation/data/raw/")  # 2. Time-to-event analysis km <- km_estimate(sim, endpoint = "stage2", stratify_by = "scenario") plot_km(km, title = "Time-to-stage-2")  # 3. Policy effect (policy A vs. policy B) pol <- policy_effect(sim, policy_a_scenario = "A-S08",                      policy_b_scenario = "A-S07") cat(pol$narrative)   # ready to drop into a report  # 4. Autonomy-level trade-off al <- al_efficiency(sim) plot_al_tradeoff(al)  # 5. Manuscript export export_latex_table(   data     = pol$effect_sizes,   filename = "policy_table.tex",   caption  = "Policy effect sizes.",   label    = "policy" )  # 6. Interactive Shiny dashboard launch_app()

title: dynasimR - Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis
text: R >= 4.1.0 (base pipe |> used throughout) Required imports: dplyr, tidyr, purrr, readr, tibble, ggplot2, survival, rlang, cli, glue Shiny app requires Suggests: dependencies — install dynasimR::check_app_dependencies()

title: dynasimR - Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis
text: MIT (c) R. Heller

what: Compute AL-Efficiency ratio (event reduction vs. compliance) — al_efficiency
title: Compute AL-Efficiency ratio (event reduction vs. compliance) — al_efficiency
text: Quantifies trade-event-rate reduction Compliance Index across autonomy levels AL0-AL5. Returns trade-table, optimal AL level (highest event reduction still compliance threshold) compliance violations.

title: Compute AL-Efficiency ratio (event reduction vs. compliance) — al_efficiency
code: al_efficiency(   data,   al_scenarios = c(`0` = "A-S00", `1` = "A-S05", `2` = "A-S09", `3` = "A-S10", `4` =     "A-S11", `5` = "A-S12"),   compliance_threshold = 0.8,   reference_al = 0,   n_bootstrap = 1000 )

title: Compute AL-Efficiency ratio (event reduction vs. compliance) — al_efficiency
text: data dynasimR_data object summary tibble. al_scenarios Named character vector. Mapping AL level (string) scenario ID. Default assumes Profile scenarios ("-" prefix). compliance_threshold Numeric. Minimum Compliance Index AL level considered acceptable. Default 0.80. reference_al Integer. AL level used reference delta calculation. Default 0. n_bootstrap Integer. Bootstrap replicates CIs. Default 1000.

title: Compute AL-Efficiency ratio (event reduction vs. compliance) — al_efficiency
text: S3 object class dynasimR_al_analysis slots tradeoff_table, optimal_al, compliance_violations params.

title: Compute AL-Efficiency ratio (event reduction vs. compliance) — al_efficiency
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sim <- load_example_data() al  <- al_efficiency(sim) plot_al_tradeoff(al) } # }

what: Check availability of Shiny-app dependencies — check_app_dependencies
title: Check availability of Shiny-app dependencies — check_app_dependencies
text: Inspects Suggests: packages required embedded Shiny dashboard prints summary ones installed.

title: Check availability of Shiny-app dependencies — check_app_dependencies
code: check_app_dependencies()

title: Check availability of Shiny-app dependencies — check_app_dependencies
text: tibble columns package, installed, version, invisibly.

title: Completion-outcome analysis (multinomial) — compute_completion_analysis
text: Fits multinomial logistic regression completion outcome (categorical) scenario + covariates. Returns model, tidied coefficients, class-level proportions.

title: Completion-outcome analysis (multinomial) — compute_completion_analysis
code: compute_completion_analysis(data, covariates = c("scenario", "severity"))

title: Completion-outcome analysis (multinomial) — compute_completion_analysis
text: data dynasimR_data object entity tibble completion_outcome column (factor). covariates Character vector. Additional predictors. Default c("scenario", "severity").

title: Completion-outcome analysis (multinomial) — compute_completion_analysis
text: S3 object class dynasimR_completion slots fit, tidy, proportions, params.

title: Compliance Index — compute_compliance_index
text: Fraction entities (irrespective group) receive service within defined time window. value 0.80 treated critical compliance violation.

title: Compliance Index — compute_compliance_index
code: compute_compliance_index(   entities,   window_min = 60,   window_unit = c("min", "hours", "days"),   by_scenario = TRUE,   by_group = TRUE,   n_bootstrap = 1000 )

title: Compliance Index — compute_compliance_index
text: entities dynasimR_data object, entity-level tibble/data.frame time_to_first_service column (Profile ) wait_days_to_min column (Profile B). window_min Numeric. Window size, expressed unit given window_unit. Default 60. window_unit Character. One "min", "hours", "days". Values internally normalised minutes. by_scenario Logical. Compute per scenario. Default TRUE. by_group Logical. Stratify group column present. Default TRUE. n_bootstrap Integer. Bootstrap replicates CIs. Default 1000.

title: Compliance Index — compute_compliance_index
text: tibble columns scenario (stratified), group (stratified), ci, ci_ci_lo, ci_ci_hi, n_total, n_in_window, compliance_critical, window_min.

title: Compliance Index — compute_compliance_index
text: Profile B data analogous metric uses wait_days 14-day window (window_unit = "days").

title: Compliance Index — compute_compliance_index
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sim <- load_example_data() ci  <- compute_compliance_index(sim) } # }

title: Wait-Gap Index (Profile B) — compute_wait_gap_index
text: Analogue Compliance Index entity-flow profiles: fraction entities whose wait time arrival activation exceed threshold number days (default 14). value 0.80 flagged critical wait-gap.

title: Wait-Gap Index (Profile B) — compute_wait_gap_index
code: compute_wait_gap_index(   data,   window_days = 14,   by_scenario = TRUE,   by_cohort = TRUE,   n_bootstrap = 1000 )

title: Wait-Gap Index (Profile B) — compute_wait_gap_index
text: data dynasimR_data object entity tibble wait_days column (wait_days_to_min minutes). window_days Numeric. Allowed wait time days. Default 14. by_scenario Logical. Stratify scenario. Default TRUE. by_cohort Logical. Stratify cohort (present). Default TRUE. n_bootstrap Integer. Bootstrap replicates CIs. Default 1000.

title: Wait-Gap Index (Profile B) — compute_wait_gap_index
text: tibble columns scenario (stratified), cohort (stratified), wgi, wgi_ci_lo, wgi_ci_hi, n_total, n_in_window, wgi_critical, window_days.

what: Cox proportional-hazards model for simulation data — cox_model
title: Cox proportional-hazards model for simulation data — cox_model
text: Cox proportional-hazards model simulation data

title: Cox proportional-hazards model for simulation data — cox_model
code: cox_model(   data,   endpoint = c("stage2", "overall", "service"),   covariates = c("scenario", "severity", "group"),   reference_scenario = "A-S00" )

title: Cox proportional-hazards model for simulation data — cox_model
text: data dynasimR_data object entity tibble. endpoint See km_estimate(). covariates Character vector. Covariates entering Cox model. Default c("scenario","severity","group"). reference_scenario Character. Reference level scenario factor. Default "-S00". Use "B-S00" Profile B.

title: Cox proportional-hazards model for simulation data — cox_model
text: S3 object class dynasimR_cox slots fit, tidy, ph_test, forest_data params.

what: Bottleneck detection across processing stages — detect_bottlenecks
title: Bottleneck detection across processing stages — detect_bottlenecks
text: Identifies scenario-stage combinations median transit time exceeds percentile threshold relative grand distribution.

title: Bottleneck detection across processing stages — detect_bottlenecks
code: detect_bottlenecks(data, threshold = 0.75)

title: Bottleneck detection across processing stages — detect_bottlenecks
text: data dynasimR_data object throughput tibble stage_throughput(). threshold Numeric. Quantile stage flagged bottleneck (0-1). Default 0.75 (top quartile).

title: Bottleneck detection across processing stages — detect_bottlenecks
text: tibble bottleneck rows.

text: domain-neutral analysis visualisation layer discrete-event, agent-based node-actor simulation outputs. package schema-harmonised two interchangeable output profiles (Profile Profile B) can analysed single API.

title: dynasimR: Dynamic Agent-Node Simulation Analysis — dynasimR-package
text: Data /O read_simulation(), load_example_data(), validate_dynasimR_data() Time--event km_estimate(), cox_model() Policy policy_effect() Autonomy al_efficiency() Compliance compute_compliance_index() Entity flow stage_throughput(), detect_bottlenecks() Resource resource_comparison() Sensitivity morris_screening(), tornado_data() Profile B helpers progress_trajectory(), compute_wait_gap_index(), group_effect(), spatial_supply_demand(), compute_completion_analysis() Plots plot_km(), plot_forest(), plot_al_tradeoff(), plot_scenario_heatmap(), plot_policy(), plot_timeline(), plot_map(), plot_progress_curves(), plot_sdi_map(), plot_cost_effectiveness() Export export_figure(), export_latex_table(), fill_placeholders() Shiny launch_app(), check_app_dependencies()

what: Export a ggplot in publication-ready format — export_figure
title: Export a ggplot in publication-ready format — export_figure
text: Writes ggplot2 object PDF/SVG/PNG/TIFF/EPS specified physical dimensions (millimetres) resolution.

title: Export a ggplot in publication-ready format — export_figure
code: export_figure(plot, filename, width_mm = 174, height_mm = 120, dpi = 300)

title: Export a ggplot in publication-ready format — export_figure
text: plot ggplot2 object. filename Character. Output path (extension determines format). width_mm Numeric. Width mm. 174 = single-column, 88 = double-column. Default 174. height_mm Numeric. Height mm. Default 120. dpi Integer. Raster resolution. Default 300.

title: Export a ggplot in publication-ready format — export_figure
text: Invisibly, filename.

title: Export a ggplot in publication-ready format — export_figure
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ p <- ggplot2::ggplot(mtcars, ggplot2::aes(mpg, hp)) +   ggplot2::geom_point() export_figure(p, "mtcars.pdf", width_mm = 120, height_mm = 90) } # }

what: Export a tibble as a publication-quality LaTeX table — export_latex_table
title: Export a tibble as a publication-quality LaTeX table — export_latex_table
text: Produces booktabs-style table using \\botrule instead \\bottomrule escaping </> cells. Optionally appends tablenotes footnote.

title: Export a tibble as a publication-quality LaTeX table — export_latex_table
code: export_latex_table(   data,   filename,   caption,   label,   note = NULL,   digits = 3,   col_format = NULL )

title: Export a tibble as a publication-quality LaTeX table — export_latex_table
text: data tibble/data.frame. filename Character. Output .tex path. caption Character. Table caption. label Character. LaTeX label (without tab: prefix; added). note Character. Optional footnote text. digits Integer. Digits numeric columns. Default 3. col_format Character vector. Column alignment spec (e.g. c("l", "r", "r")). Default NULL picks "l" first column "r" rest.

title: Export a tibble as a publication-quality LaTeX table — export_latex_table
text: LaTeX source, invisibly.

title: Export a tibble as a publication-quality LaTeX table — export_latex_table
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ export_latex_table(head(iris), "iris.tex",                    caption = "First rows of iris.",                    label   = "iris") } # }

what: Fill [XX_*] placeholders in a LaTeX manuscript — fill_placeholders
title: Fill [XX_*] placeholders in a LaTeX manuscript — fill_placeholders
text: Substitutes [XX_*] placeholders LaTeX source file automatically computed simulation statistics. mapping placeholder computation assembled inside function policy_effect(), al_efficiency() compute_compliance_index().

title: Fill [XX_*] placeholders in a LaTeX manuscript — fill_placeholders
code: fill_placeholders(   sim_data,   tex_file,   output_file = NULL,   dry_run = FALSE,   policy_a_scenario = "A-S08",   policy_b_scenario = "A-S07",   baseline_scenario = "A-S00" )

title: Fill [XX_*] placeholders in a LaTeX manuscript — fill_placeholders
text: sim_data dynasimR_data object. tex_file Character. Path LaTeX source file. output_file Character. Output path; default appends _filled .tex. dry_run Logical. TRUE, print replacements write file. Default FALSE. policy_a_scenario Character. Policy scenario ID. Default "-S08". policy_b_scenario Character. Policy B scenario ID. Default "-S07". baseline_scenario Character. Baseline scenario ID. Default "-S00".

title: Fill [XX_*] placeholders in a LaTeX manuscript — fill_placeholders
text: Named character vector replacements (invisibly).

title: Between-group prioritisation effect (Profile B) — group_effect
text: Compares two Profile B scenarios differ inclusion rules groups prioritised, returns completion rates, cost per completion difference (delta) bootstrap CIs.

title: Between-group prioritisation effect (Profile B) — group_effect
code: group_effect(   data,   group_a_scenario,   group_b_scenario,   outcome_col = "completion_rate",   cost_col = "cost",   alpha = 0.05,   n_bootstrap = 1000 )

title: Between-group prioritisation effect (Profile B) — group_effect
text: data dynasimR_data object summary tibble. group_a_scenario Character. Scenario ID group priority. group_b_scenario Character. Scenario ID group B priority. outcome_col Character. Column name completion outcome. Default "completion_rate". cost_col Character. Column name cost metric. Default "cost". alpha Numeric. Significance level. Default 0.05. n_bootstrap Integer. Bootstrap replicates. Default 1000.

title: Between-group prioritisation effect (Profile B) — group_effect
text: S3 object class dynasimR_group_effect slots completion, cost, delta_completion, delta_cost, params.

what: Kaplan-Meier estimator for simulation data — km_estimate
title: Kaplan-Meier estimator for simulation data — km_estimate
text: Estimates survival functions simulated entity outcomes. Supports several endpoints output profiles.

title: Kaplan-Meier estimator for simulation data — km_estimate
code: km_estimate(   data,   scenarios = NULL,   endpoint = c("stage2", "overall", "service", "regression", "completion", "phase_c"),   stratify_by = "scenario",   ci_method = "log",   n_bootstrap = 0,   seed = 42 )

title: Kaplan-Meier estimator for simulation data — km_estimate
text: data dynasimR_data object tibble/data.frame containing entity-level columns. scenarios Character vector. Restrict scenario IDs. Default NULL = . endpoint Character. One "stage2", "overall", "service", "regression", "completion", "phase_c". latter three Profile-B-endpoints. stratify_by Character vector. Strata variables, e.g. "scenario" c("scenario", "group"). ci_method Character. Passed survival::survfit() conf.type. Default "log". n_bootstrap Integer. Bootstrap replicates KM CIs (0 = disabled). Default 0. seed Integer. RNG seed bootstrap. Default 42.

title: Kaplan-Meier estimator for simulation data — km_estimate
text: S3 object class dynasimR_km (list) slots fit, tidy, logrank, median_survival, boot_ci params.

title: Kaplan-Meier estimator for simulation data — km_estimate
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ sim <- load_example_data() km <- km_estimate(sim, endpoint = "stage2",                   stratify_by = "scenario") print(km) plot_km(km) } # }

what: Launch the dynasimR Shiny dashboard — launch_app
title: Launch the dynasimR Shiny dashboard — launch_app
text: Starts interactive analysis app bundled dynasimR. app requires simulation data; either supply directory via data_dir leave NULL use bundled example data.

title: Launch the dynasimR Shiny dashboard — launch_app
code: launch_app(data_dir = NULL, port = 3838, launch.browser = TRUE, ...)

title: Launch the dynasimR Shiny dashboard — launch_app
text: data_dir Character. Path directory simulation outputs. Default NULL = use bundled example data. port Integer. Port local server. Default 3838. launch.browser Logical. Open browser. Default TRUE. ... Additional arguments forwarded shiny::runApp().

title: Launch the dynasimR Shiny dashboard — launch_app
text: return (blocks app closed).

title: Launch the dynasimR Shiny dashboard — launch_app
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ launch_app() launch_app(data_dir = "~/my-simulation/data/raw/") } # }

what: Morris-style sensitivity screening from simulation replicates — morris_screening
title: Morris-style sensitivity screening from simulation replicates — morris_screening
text: Uses per-replicate variation screen influential input parameters outcome metric. implementation simple variance-based screening; full Morris design use sensitivity package directly pass results visualisation.

title: Morris-style sensitivity screening from simulation replicates — morris_screening

Default NULL loads every scenario found data_dir. validate Logical. Run validate_dynasimR_data() loaded tables. Default TRUE. verbose Logical. Print progress messages. Default TRUE.

Default "supply". demand_col Character. Demand column name. Default "demand". eps Numeric. Offset avoid division zero. Default 1.

title: dynasimR 0.1.0
text: Initial release.

title: dynasimR 0.1.0
text: Load validate simulation outputs two interchangeable profiles via read_simulation() / load_example_data(). Time--event analysis km_estimate(), cox_model(), plot_km(), plot_forest(). Policy-effect quantification via policy_effect() auto-generated LaTeX-ready narrative. Autonomy-level trade-analysis via al_efficiency() plot_al_tradeoff(). Compliance Index via compute_compliance_index() bootstrap confidence intervals. Profile B helpers: progress_trajectory(), compute_wait_gap_index(), group_effect(), spatial_supply_demand(), compute_completion_analysis(). Manuscript export: export_figure(), export_latex_table(), fill_placeholders() (publisher-template compatible). Embedded Shiny dashboard via launch_app(). Example data shipped reproducible demos tests.
